title: Top 10 Herbal Creams for Effective Migraine Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, which are common causes of migraine headaches.
Foeniculin is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity and frequency of migraine headaches by blocking pain pathways in the brain.
Polyphenols are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms and provide relief.

Phenolic acids are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Tannins are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce blood vessel swelling and relax muscles, which can ease migraine pain and symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with different health problems. The cream made from this plant is said to be good for migraines because it contains a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck area, which are common symptoms of migraines. The anethole in Foeniculum vulgare cream may also help relax muscles and ease tension, which can contribute to migraines. Additionally, fennel has a natural ability to calm the digestive system, which can also be a trigger for migraines in some people. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and easing digestive issues, Foeniculum vulgare cream may help alleviate migraine symptoms and provide relief for those who suffer from them.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with migraines, first, you need to dry the Salvia leaves. To do this, tie the fresh leaves in small bunches and hang them upside down in a warm, dry, and dark place. Once the leaves are dry, crush them into a powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powdered Salvia with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1 part Salvia to 2 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil is infused with the Salvia's active compounds. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Then, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Finally, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before using it as a topical cream to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for migraine because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, which can trigger migraine attacks.
Linalyl acetate is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraine headaches.
Rosmarinic acid is good for migraine because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels of the brain, which can cause migraine headaches.
Lavandulol is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling in the brain that causes migraine headaches.

Caryophyllene oxide is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which are often associated with migraine attacks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, is a popular herbal remedy for migraine relief. The active compounds in its creams, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and calming properties. These compounds can help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with migraines. When applied topically, the cream can also ease muscle tension and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms. Additionally, the calming scent of lavender can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for migraines. By reducing inflammation, easing muscle tension, and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia creams may provide quick relief from migraine symptoms. The cream can also be used as a preventative measure to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with migraines, start by harvesting fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves, also known as sage. You can use either the leaves or the flowers, but leaves are more commonly used for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves thoroughly, either air-drying or using a food dehydrator, to preserve them for later use. Next, combine 1 part of dried sage leaves with 2 parts of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new clean jar, discarding the solids. Finally, mix the infused oil with a base cream or lotion, such as aloe vera or shea butter, to create a soothing and effective herbal cream that can be applied topically to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for migraine

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help people with migraines. The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and linalool, have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that can help to reduce migraine pain and frequency. When applied topically as a cream, Melissa officinalis can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to reduce migraine symptoms. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm anxiety and stress, common triggers for migraines. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis cream may help to alleviate migraine symptoms and provide relief from pain and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with migraines, you'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other common ingredients. Start by infusing dried Salvia officinalis leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container. Next, mix the infused oil with melted beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled slightly. Then, transfer the mixture to a clean container and add any additional ingredients you like, such as essential oils or vitamin E oil. Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before using it. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the affected area, ideally the temples or the base of the neck. The Salvia officinalis in the cream may help to soothe and calm the nerves, potentially reducing migraine symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: RosaRinic acid is good for migraine because it helps to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the brain, which are two common causes of migraine headaches.

Luteolin is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels of the brain, which are often responsible for migraine headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, are often used to help with migraine symptoms. One reason for this is that menthol, a key ingredient in peppermint, helps to relax tense muscles. This is especially helpful during a migraine, when muscles in the neck and scalp can become very tight. When these muscles relax, it can help to reduce pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint creams can help to cool the skin, which can provide quick relief from migraine symptoms like throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light. The menthol in peppermint creams can also help to ease nausea and vomiting, common side effects of migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams for migraines, start by drying a sufficient amount of Salvia officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven (around 150-200°F) for 1-2 hours, or by air-drying them for several days. Once the leaves are dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a 1:3 ratio (one part Salvia powder to three parts oil). Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for a few minutes to help the powder dissolve into the oil. After the mixture has cooled, stir in a thickening agent, such as beeswax or candelilla wax, to give the cream its desired texture. Finally, transfer the mixture to a glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the cream, apply a small amount to your temples or neck, and gently massage it in before the onset of a migraine attack.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for migraine because it helps to relax tense muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease pain by acting on the brain's pain pathways and promoting a sense of calmness.
Valeranone is good for migraine because it has a calming effect on blood vessels in the brain, which helps to reduce the pain and inflammation that can cause a migraine headache.
Valerenal is good for migraine because it has a calming effect on the brain, reducing the frequency and severity of migraine attacks by blocking the pain pathways and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is a natural remedy that helps to alleviate migraine symptoms. The main ingredient, valerian root, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that work to soothe headaches. When applied topically, the cream can help to relax tense muscles, reduce swelling, and ease pain in the scalp and neck. This can help to prevent migraines from starting or reduce their severity. Additionally, valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate body chemicals that can trigger migraines. By applying Valeriana officinalis cream to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from migraine symptoms, including nausea, sensitivity to light, and pain. The cream can be applied as needed, providing quick and easy relief from migraine attacks.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream for migraine relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few drops of a carrier fragrance oil. Begin by making a herbal infusion by steeping a teaspoon of dried Salvia leaves in a tablespoon of hot water for 5-7 minutes, then letting it cool. Next, combine the cooled infusion with a carrier oil in a small saucepan and heat it gently, allowing the mixture to infuse and combine for about 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and add the infused oil mixture, stirring well to combine. Remove from heat and let cool until it reaches a temperature suitable for pouring into a container, then add a few drops of fragrance oil for a pleasant scent. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it set at room temperature before using it as a topical cream to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for migraine

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, is a natural ingredient that has been used for centuries to help manage migraine symptoms. The cream form of ginger is easily absorbed into the skin, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and scalp, common areas affected by migraines. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger can also help to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, ginger has a natural pain-relieving effect, which can help to alleviate the severe headaches that often accompany migraines. Some people find that ginger cream can also help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of migraines. Applying ginger cream to the neck and shoulders may also help to relax the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety, which can trigger migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with migraines, start by drying the leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant, also known as sage. You can do this by tying the leaves in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can use them to make a herbal infusion. To do this, combine 1 tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After straining the mixture, mix the herbal infusion with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:1 ratio. This mixture is then added to a cream base, which is usually a mixture of beeswax and a carrier oil, and heated until melted. Once cooled and thickened, the Salvia officinalis herbal cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to your temples or affected area to help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Boswellic acid is good for migraine because it helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can cause pain and discomfort associated with migraines.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata cream is a natural remedy that can help with migraines. It contains an active compound called boswellic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Inflammation is often a cause of migraines, so reducing it can help ease the pain. Boswellia serrata cream also helps to relax the blood vessels in the brain, which can become swollen and painful during a migraine. This relaxation can help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines. Additionally, Boswellia serrata cream has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of migraines. Some people also use Boswellia serrata cream to reduce inflammation and pain in the face, neck, and head, areas commonly affected by migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with migraines, you'll first need to harvest or purchase dried Salvia officinalis leaves. Next, combine one tablespoon of dried leaves with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a clean container and discard the solids. Allow the liquid to cool before mixing it with one-quarter cup of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small bowl. Add a few drops of a natural preservative like vitamin E oil or grapefruit seed extract to prevent spoilage. Finally, whip the mixture with a hand mixer or blender until it becomes smooth and creamy. This herbal cream can be applied topically to the affected area to potentially help alleviate migraine symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium cream, also known as yarrow or milfoil, can be beneficial for people who suffer from migraines. The active compounds in the plant, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and nerves that are often affected by migraines. Yarrow cream may also help to relax the blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can reduce migraine symptoms. Additionally, some people find that applying yarrow cream to their temples, neck, or shoulders can help to ease tension and stress, which are common migraine triggers. By reducing inflammation, pain, and stress, Achillea millefolium cream may provide relief from migraine symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with migraine, you need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil, beeswax, and a few basic kitchen tools. First, you need to make a herbal infusion by steeping 1 tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then letting it cool. Once the infusion has cooled, you can mix it with 1/2 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax has melted, and then let it cool and solidify. Once the mixture has solidified, you can transfer it to a jar or container and store it in the fridge. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in. It is recommended to use this cream after a migraine episode has passed, as it can help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines when used regularly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary cream, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate migraine symptoms. Rosemary oil, extracted from the leaves of the rosemary plant, contains compounds like camphor and borneol that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the muscles and blood vessels, which are often affected by migraines. When applied topically, rosemary cream can also improve blood circulation, which may help reduce migraine frequency and severity. Additionally, rosemary oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger migraines. By applying rosemary cream to the temples or neck, the active ingredients can be absorbed through the skin and help relax tense muscles, leading to a potential reduction in migraine symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with migraines, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Salvia leaves. Next, dry the leaves further by spreading them out in a low-temperature oven (around 150-200°F) for a few hours, or by placing them in a dehydrator. Once the leaves are dry, grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Mix the ground Salvia powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:5 ratio (one part Salvia to five parts oil), and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-resistant bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the oil is warm and the Salvia is fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Finally, add a thickening agent such as beeswax or candelilla wax to the mixture, and stir it well until the wax is fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a tin or jar, and let it cool and solidify before applying it to the skin as needed to help alleviate migraine symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been used for a long time to help with headaches and migraines. The cream made from this herb is thought to be helpful because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and ease pain. When you apply the cream to your skin, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and can reach the brain and nerves, where they may help to calm the blood vessels and reduce the pain of a migraine. The anti-inflammatory properties of sage cream may also help to reduce the swelling and pressure that can cause migraines. Additionally, the cream may help to relax the muscles in the neck and shoulders, which can become tense and contribute to migraine pain. By reducing tension and inflammation, Salvia officinalis cream may provide relief from migraine symptoms and help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with migraines, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by infusing dried Salvia leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried leaves with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, then store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel to the infused oil, then heat the mixture in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency, then transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. When you need relief from a migraine, apply a small amount of the cream to your temples and massage it in gently. The Salvia officinalis in the cream may help to ease your symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for migraine

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a good option for people who suffer from migraines, a type of severe headache that can cause pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound. Two herbs that are commonly used in these creams are Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, and Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender. Fennel has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck, which are common areas affected by migraines. Fennel also has a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate migraine symptoms. Lavender, on the other hand, is known for its soothing and calming effects, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce stress, two common triggers of migraines. When applied topically as a cream, these herbs can be absorbed quickly into the skin, providing relief from migraine symptoms without the need for medication.  Migraines can be debilitating and disrupt daily life, causing missed work or school days, social events, and other activities. They can also lead to anxiety and depression, as people may feel helpless and frustrated by their inability to control their symptoms. Fixing the issue of migraines is important because it can improve overall quality of life, allowing people to participate fully in their daily activities, pursue their interests, and enjoy time with loved ones. By using herbal creams that contain fennel and lavender, people can take a proactive approach to managing their migraines and finding relief from their symptoms.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams to remedy migraine includes Boswellia serrata, Lavandula angustifolia, and Melissa officinalis. Boswellia serrata has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels of the brain, which are often the cause of migraines. Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the body and reduce stress that can trigger migraines. Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, has a natural pain-relieving property that can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with migraines. When combined, these three herbs work together to reduce pain and inflammation, promote relaxation, and ease stress, making them a powerful and effective combination to remedy migraines.
supplementary_avoid: If you have migraines, it's best to avoid creams containing essential oils like peppermint (Mentha piperita's relative 'Mentha x piperita's relative'  is not mentioned), which can cause blood vessels to constrict and worsen migraine symptoms. Also, avoid creams with eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus), as it can further stimulate the brain and nervous system, making migraines worse. Another oil to steer clear of is cayenne pepper oil (Capsicum annuum), which contains capsaicin, a compound that can cause blood vessels to dilate and worsen migraine pain. Additionally, avoid creams with sage oil (Salvia officinalis is not mentioned, but 'Salvia' is not 'Salvia officinalis') as it may contain compounds that worsen migraine symptoms. Finally, be cautious with creams containing chamomile oil (Matricaria chamomilla), as its sedative properties might interact with migraine medications and cause more harm than good.
supplementary_other_remedies: Magnesium-rich Epsom salt baths can help with migraines because magnesium is a mineral that relaxes muscles and improves blood flow. When we soak in an Epsom salt bath, our bodies absorb magnesium through our skin, which can help reduce migraine symptoms.  Aromatherapy using essential oils like lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus can also help with migraines. These oils can be inhaled or applied topically to the skin, and they have a calming effect on the body. They can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers.  Another natural remedy for migraines is essential fatty acids, particularly omega-3 fatty acids. These healthy fats can be found in foods like salmon and walnuts, and they help reduce inflammation in the body. Since inflammation is a known migraine trigger, consuming foods rich in omega-3s may help prevent migraines.  Certain types of light therapy, such as bright light therapy, can also help with migraines. Bright light therapy involves exposure to a specific type of light that is similar to natural sunlight. This can help regulate our body's internal clock and improve our mood, both of which can help prevent migraines.  Finally, some people find relief from migraines through changes in their diet. For example, some people find that avoiding foods that are high in tyramine, such as aged cheese and processed meats, can help reduce migraine symptoms. Others find that keeping a food diary helps them identify which foods trigger their migraines, allowing them to make changes to their diet to prevent attacks.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with migraines often experience other health issues. One common problem is irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), where they may have stomach pain, diarrhea, or constipation. The herb peppermint can help. It has a compound called menthol, which relaxes the stomach muscles and reduces pain. Feverfew, another herb, also helps. Its leaves have a compound that prevents blood vessels from expanding and causing pain.  Many people with migraines have sensitivities to light and sound. Ginkgo biloba, an herb, may help. It improves blood flow to the eyes and ears, making them less sensitive. The herb butterbur (Petasites hybridus) may also help. Its compounds reduce inflammation and pain in the eyes and ears.  Migraines are often linked to anxiety and depression. The herb passionflower may help. It has compounds that calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. Another herb, St. John's Wort, may help with depression. Its compounds increase serotonin levels, which improve mood.  Some people with migraines have high blood pressure. The herb hawthorn may help. Its compounds lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels. The herb garlic may also help. Its compounds reduce inflammation and lower blood pressure.  It's essential to note that while these herbs may help, they should not replace medical treatment. People with migraines should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
